questions.q: Rakesh Sharma is celebrated as the first Indian citizen to do what?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma became the first Indian citizen to travel into space in 1984.

questions.q: In which year did Rakesh Sharma fly into space?
questions.explanation: He flew aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 mission in April 1984.

questions.q: Which branch of the Indian armed forces did Rakesh Sharma serve in?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma was a pilot in the Indian Air Force before becoming a cosmonaut.

questions.q: When India's Prime Minister asked how the country looked from space, Rakesh Sharma famously replied with which phrase?
questions.explanation: He replied 'Saare Jahan Se Achha' (better than the whole world) when asked how India looked from orbit.

questions.q: With which country's space programme did Rakesh Sharma fly into orbit?
questions.explanation: His spaceflight was a joint Indo-Soviet mission flown on a Soviet spacecraft.

questions.q: The spacecraft that carried Rakesh Sharma to orbit belonged to which family of vehicles?
questions.explanation: He launched aboard the Soyuz T-11 spacecraft.

questions.q: Which Indian Prime Minister spoke to Rakesh Sharma during his spaceflight?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister who spoke with him during the 1984 mission.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma is regarded as India's first what?
questions.explanation: He was India's first cosmonaut (astronaut), the first of his nation to reach space.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Rakesh Sharma spend in space during his mission?
questions.explanation: He spent about a week (just under eight days) in space.

questions.q: What was Rakesh Sharma's primary profession before being selected for the space mission?
questions.explanation: He was a fighter pilot and test pilot in the Indian Air Force.

questions.q: Which exact Soviet spacecraft carried Rakesh Sharma into orbit in 1984?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma launched aboard Soyuz T-11 on 3 April 1984.

questions.q: Which orbiting space station did Rakesh Sharma's crew dock with and live aboard?
questions.explanation: His Soyuz T-11 docked with the Soviet Salyut 7 space station.

questions.q: In which month and year did Rakesh Sharma's spaceflight take place?
questions.explanation: The Soyuz T-11 mission launched on 3 April 1984.

questions.q: What military rank did Rakesh Sharma hold in the Indian Air Force at the time of his spaceflight?
questions.explanation: He flew the mission as a Squadron Leader in the Indian Air Force.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma was awarded India's highest peacetime gallantry honour. Which award was it?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Ashoka Chakra, India's highest peacetime gallantry decoration.

questions.q: The Soviet commander of Rakesh Sharma's Soyuz T-11 mission was which cosmonaut?
questions.explanation: Yuri Malyshev commanded the Soyuz T-11 flight that carried Rakesh Sharma.

questions.q: The Indo-Soviet mission that took Rakesh Sharma to space was part of which Soviet cooperation programme?
questions.explanation: His flight was part of the Soviet Interkosmos programme, which flew cosmonauts from allied nations.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma practised which discipline in orbit to study its effects in microgravity, an experiment widely publicised in India?
questions.explanation: He performed yoga in orbit to study how the practice might counter the effects of weightlessness.

questions.q: After leaving the Air Force, Rakesh Sharma worked as a test pilot for which Indian aerospace organisation?
questions.explanation: He later served as a chief test pilot at Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).

questions.q: Which Indian state was Rakesh Sharma born in?
questions.explanation: Rakesh Sharma was born in Patiala, Punjab, in 1949.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma's flight made India the nth country to send a citizen into space. Approximately which position did India take?
questions.explanation: India became roughly the fourteenth nation to have one of its citizens travel to space.

questions.q: Who was Rakesh Sharma's designated backup cosmonaut for the 1984 mission?
questions.explanation: Ravish Malhotra, also an Indian Air Force pilot, was the backup cosmonaut for the mission.

questions.q: On what date did the Soyuz T-11 mission carrying Rakesh Sharma lift off?
questions.explanation: Soyuz T-11 launched on 3 April 1984 from the Baikonur Cosmodrome.

questions.q: Besides commander Malyshev and Rakesh Sharma, who was the third crew member on the Soyuz T-11 launch?
questions.explanation: Gennady Strekalov served as the flight engineer on the three-person Soyuz T-11 crew alongside commander Yuri Malyshev and Rakesh Sharma.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma's crew launched on Soyuz T-11 but returned to Earth aboard a different spacecraft. Which one?
questions.explanation: Following standard station crew-exchange practice, the visiting crew returned in the earlier-docked Soyuz T-10 descent module.

questions.q: From which Soviet launch site did Rakesh Sharma's Soyuz T-11 mission lift off?
questions.explanation: The mission launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.

questions.q: Along with the Ashoka Chakra, Rakesh Sharma received which Soviet honour after his flight?
questions.explanation: The Soviet Union conferred the title Hero of the Soviet Union on Rakesh Sharma.

questions.q: Rakesh Sharma was selected as a cosmonaut candidate in which year, a couple of years before his flight?
questions.explanation: He was selected for the joint Indo-Soviet space programme in 1982, training before the 1984 flight.

questions.q: During the mission, Rakesh Sharma carried out a multi-spectral photography programme focused on which region of India?
questions.explanation: His Earth-observation work included multi-spectral photography of northern India, notably the Himalayan region, to aid future hydroelectric and resource studies.

questions.q: As of the decades following his flight, Rakesh Sharma's status among Indian-born space travellers can best be described as which of the following?
options: One of dozens of Indian citizens to fly to space
The first Indian to walk on the Moon
The first Indian to fly on the US Space Shuttle
The first and, for many years, the only Indian citizen to fly to space
questions.explanation: He remained the first and, for many years, the only Indian citizen to have travelled to space (Kalpana Chawla flew as a US citizen).
